element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic: striking enemies to inflate them for platform/projectile use
Timing-based mechanic: must not stay on inflated enemy too long or it explodes; used for vertical traversal
Directional physics: push inflated enemies to ricochet and damage other enemies or protagonist
Resource management: scattered throughout levels, obtained by striking special Upa-face blocks
Persistent upgrade: increases maximum health by one unit for current world duration
Optional collectible: obtained from special blocks, no mechanical impact
Temporary crowd control: freezes all enemies on-screen for brief duration
Power-up: grants temporary invincibility and running ability for increased mobility
Advanced utility: pushes off platform to hit ground, killing all on-screen enemies

typical_relief_triggers: Finding milk bottles to restore health
Using hourglasses to freeze threatening enemies
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based tension with health meter as primary stress indicator; relief gates tied to item discovery; escalating enemy complexity through world progression

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Special Upa-face blocks reveal hidden milk bottles, hearts, and power-ups when struck; incentivizes exploration and strikes
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Scorpion blocks appear to be hidden or contextual; vertical scroll levels (BGM3) suggest multi-screen vertical navigation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of rattle mechanics and basic enemy inflation for platforms; safe health pool with frequent milk access
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Inflated enemy weapon ricocheting introduced; power-up variety expands (hourglasses, bells); spatial complexity increases with vertical scroll sections and underwater sections
difficulty_curve.late_game: Scorpion block mechanics; compound enemy patterns; reduced health item frequency; final boss encounter
notable_spikes: Transition from platform-use to weapon-use mechanics (conceptual complexity spike)
Introduction of vertical scroll levels (spatial disorientation)
Underwater level sections (altered physics, BGM4 context)
Final boss encounter (confirmed by BGM track list)
Famicom Normal mode: reduced starting health/lives and increased enemy damage vs. Easy mode

notable_level_patterns.description: Multi-screen vertical navigation levels signaled by dedicated BGM3 track; requires mastery of inflated enemy platforms for height traversal
Alternate physics environment with dedicated BGM4 track; distinct atmosphere and enemy behavior patterns
Game structured in worlds with stage/level subdivisions; world clear screen transitions trigger difficulty escalation; heart power-ups reset per world
Special Upa-face blocks unlock resources; encourages systematic exploration and rattle use as problem-solving tool beyond combat

sources_used: Wikipedia - Bio Miracle Bokutte Upa (Gameplay section, Release history, Story)
The Cutting Room Floor - Bio Miracle Bokutte Upa (Version Differences, Music tracks documentation)
